reasoning: Beyond Meat has $1.15 billion in convertible notes, but these are 0% coupon and do not mature until March 15, 2027. Because there are no cash interest payments required and the maturity date is well past the Jan 1, 2026 resolution date, there is no forced debt-related trigger for bankruptcy in 2025. Furthermore, the company reported $145.6 million in cash and cash equivalents at the end of 2024, supported by an active ATM equity offering program. Q4 2024 showed operational improvement, with gross margins turning positive (13.1%) and adjusted EBITDA losses narrowing significantly to $26 million. This gives them ample liquidity to survive 2025 without completely running out of cash. While bondholders have engaged restructuring advisors (Akin Gump) to negotiate a solution for the 2027 debt wall, Beyond Meat has no secured debt and flexibility to pursue an out-of-court debt exchange. Although an in-court pre-packaged bankruptcy to force an exchange is a risk, the lack of near-term maturity or immediate operational insolvency makes a 2025 bankruptcy unlikely.
